children.name: id
children.title: NPC
children.tooltip: Which NPC should be in the crate.

children.name: count
children.title: Amount
children.tooltip: Number of this NPC to spawn.

children.name: stack
children.title: Stack
children.tooltip: Spawns additional NPCs in a stack. Can be positive (down) or negative (up). Useful for Pokeys or Ladders.

children.name: speed
children.title: Speed Multiplier
children.tooltip: Multiplier for the velocity of the NPC when the crate breaks.

children.name: angle
children.title: Angle
children.tooltip: Angle of the NPC on ejection.

children.name: delay
children.title: Delay
children.tooltip: Time spent intangible after coming out of the crate.

children.name: floaty
children.title: Floaty
children.tooltip: If true, the NPC has no gravity acting upon it during the intangible period.

children.name: ai1
children.title: Ai1
children.tooltip: Ai1 field of the spawned NPC.

children.name: ai2
children.title: Ai2
children.tooltip: Ai2 field of the spawned NPC.

children.name: ai3
children.title: Ai3
children.tooltip: Ai3 field of the spawned NPC.

children.name: ai4
children.title: Ai4
children.tooltip: Ai4 field of the spawned NPC.

children.name: ai5
children.title: Ai5
children.tooltip: Ai5 field of the spawned NPC.

children.name: friendly
children.title: Friendly
children.tooltip: If true, the NPC spawns as friendly.

children.name: dontMove
children.title: Don't Move
children.tooltip: If true, the NPC spawns with the Don't Move flag set.

children.name: layerName
children.title: Layer Name
children.tooltip: Layer to spawn the NPC onto.

children.name: noMoreObjInLayer
children.title: Layer is Empty Event
children.tooltip: Event to trigger when the layer is empty.

children.name: deathEventName
children.title: Death Event
children.tooltip: Event to trigger when the spawned NPC dies.

children.name: talkEventName
children.title: Talk Event
children.tooltip: Event to trigger when the NPC is talked to.

layout.name: contents
layout.title: (Legacy) Contents
layout.tooltip: Used to be used to define the crate's contents. Kept for compatibility. Use crate.lua to reference the args. Example: {2, 3} or to specify the properties of an NPC to spawn, use a table like so: {{id=2, count=5}}
